name: Fairlearn
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Microsoft project; no API costs

performance.notes: Local computation; mitigation algorithms run on training timescales

agent_readiness.notes: Microsoft's AI fairness assessment and mitigation toolkit. Metrics: demographic parity, equalized odds, calibration by group. Mitigation algorithms: ExponentiatedGradient (in-processing), ThresholdOptimizer (post-processing), CorrelationRemover (pre-processing). Dashboard visualization. Scikit-learn compatible. Used in responsible AI programs for bias auditing before model deployment.

gotchas: Fairness metrics can conflict — demographic parity vs equalized odds tradeoffs
Mitigation may reduce overall accuracy in exchange for fairness
Sensitive attribute must be known at prediction time for some algorithms
No consensus on 'correct' fairness metric — context-dependent
best_when: Auditing models for demographic bias before production deployment
Compliance with EU AI Act, EEOC, or regulatory fairness requirements
Binary classification with protected attributes (race, gender, age)
Comparing fairness-accuracy tradeoffs of different models
avoid_when: Teams without domain expertise to interpret fairness metrics
Regression fairness beyond basic demographic parity
summary: Fairlearn is Microsoft's open-source toolkit for AI fairness assessment and bias mitigation. Provides fairness metrics (demographic parity, equalized odds) and mitigation algorithms (ExponentiatedGradient, ThresholdOptimizer). Dashboard visualization of fairness-accuracy tradeoffs. Used in responsible AI programs for regulatory compliance. Best for binary classification bias auditing before model deployment.
